description: Two people are harvesting coffee cherries in a lush green coffee plantation. Both appear to be adult women wearing broad-brimmed hats and gloves; the person on the left wears a light pink jacket and beige hat, leaning forward and reaching into the coffee plants, while the person on the right wears a green jacket and blue hat, smiling and extending an arm toward the crop. The setting is outdoors in dense vegetation with large coffee leaves filling the foreground and background. Red coffee cherries are visible on branches near the left-center. The lighting is bright and natural, with a fresh, vibrant, green atmosphere. The image serves as a cover photograph for the report. The words “Creating Shared Value and Sustainability Report 2023” and “Advancing regenerative food systems at scale” are overlaid in white at the lower left. No other text, watermarks, or annotations are visible.

Circular sustainability framework infographic centered on the Nestlé logo and “Good food, Good life,” with three large colored segments labeled “PEOPLE, FAMILIES & PETS” (blue), “PEOPLE IN COMMUNITIES” (orange), and “PEOPLE & PLANET” (green). Surrounding outer ring segments are labeled “Improved nutrition,” “Tailored nutrition,” “Affordable nutrition,” “Support for balanced lifestyles,” “Packaging & circularity,” “Climate,” “Water,” “Nature & biodiversity,” “Responsible sourcing,” “Human rights,” “Diversity, equity & inclusion,” and “Youth & partners.” Small illustrations show people, pets, plants, crops, and wind turbines.

Photograph of two suited men standing indoors in front of large windows, looking at and pointing to a black folder or document. Behind them is a bright outdoor landscape with green hills, a modern building, and a Swiss flag on a pole.
Signatures of Paul Bulcke, Chairman, and U. Mark Schneider, Chief Executive Officer.
EY logo/branding
Main KPI table

Diagram showing Nestlé’s governance and implementation structure. It places the Board of Directors and Board Committees at the top, with the Executive Board below, and the CSV Council and ESG & Sustainability Council as advisory/supporting bodies. Green arrows in the key indicate Approves, lighter green indicates Reports, and brown indicates Advises.

Materiality matrix showing Nestlé topics plotted by importance and impact on business success versus impact on people and the environment, with axes labeled Negligible, Moderate, Significant, and Major. Topics are grouped across the matrix with numbered green markers and a side list of report chapters covering those topics.

A materiality matrix chart with a 4x4-style grid. The horizontal axis is “Importance and impact on Nestlé’s business success” from Negligible to Major, and the vertical axis is “Impact of Nestlé on people and the environment” from Negligible to Major. Green numbered markers label topics including governance structures & mechanisms, data privacy management, business ethics, community relations, animal welfare, employee health & safety, physical impacts of climate change, access & affordability, product labeling & marketing, circular economy, human rights & labor practices, employee acquisition, talent management & retention, employee engagement, diversity & inclusion, packaging lifecycle management, water management, ecological impacts, GHG emissions, health & nutrition, environmental & social impacts of ingredient supply chain, and product quality & safety. A side list titled “Report chapters covering topics” maps chapter names to topic numbers.

A left-to-right process flow with six steps connected by green arrows: Material topics selection; Establish external context; Stakeholder engagement; Materiality assessment; Review strategies; Publish in CSV and Sustainability Report. Supporting text under each step notes SASB-aligned material topics selected, AI software analysis and analysis of environment, 55 interviews conducted and surveys collected, quantitative and qualitative input assessed, identify risks and opportunities in results and adjust priorities accordingly, and share results internally and externally.

A woman kneels in a green agricultural field or nursery, smiling at the camera and holding a small plant. She wears a cap, white polo shirt, and jeans. Rows of plants fill the foreground and background, with a dirt path and vertical poles visible. The image is outdoors in daylight and appears to illustrate farming or stakeholder engagement in agriculture.

Photograph of three people in a lush outdoor coffee plantation or similar tropical crop field. A man in a black cap and black shirt on the left points toward the plants on the right. A woman in a white cap and beige clothing stands in the center looking toward him, and another woman in a beige hat and light pink shirt stands on the right gesturing toward the coffee shrubs. Green foliage fills the foreground and background; red coffee cherries are visible on the right. Natural daylight, no visible text overlays.

Line/area chart titled 'Nestlé GHG emissions*, GHG removals and % net reduction vs. 2018 baseline (million tonnes of CO2e)'. X-axis years 2018-2023; y-axis roughly 50-100. Shows 2018 baseline 91.17, a peak around 2020, then decline to 79.55 GHG emissions in 2023, with 78.79 net GHG emissions after 0.76 removals and 13.58% net GHG emissions reduction vs. 2018 baseline. Includes annotation 'Net Zero Roadmap launched' at 2020 and footnotes about scopes and baseline restatement.

Donut chart titled '2023 absolute CO2e reductions and removals from our interventions (million tonnes)'. Center value 13.29 million tonnes. Segments labeled Responsible Sourcing 28%, Sourcing – Dairy & Livestock 25%, Manufacturing 15%, Sourcing – Soil & Forest 9%, Packaging 7%, Logistics 4%, Recipe reformulation 6%, and Removals 6%.

Vertical SDG icon panel with standard colored tiles for SDG 7 Affordable and Clean Energy, SDG 11 Sustainable Cities and Communities, SDG 13 Climate Action, SDG 14 Life Below Water, and SDG 15 Life on Land.

Bar chart titled "Total Scope 1, 2 and 3 GHG emissions (million tonnes of CO2e)" showing annual emissions for 2021, 2022, and 2023 with Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 components, totals labeled 103.58*, 97.80*, and 87.54, in-scope values 93.29**, 88.42**, and 84.08, and excluded portions 1.44/3.38, 0.74/3.27, and 0.31/3.16. Includes a legend and a 2018 baseline annotation.
Donut chart summarizing 79.55** million tonnes and showing breakdown: 67% sourcing ingredients and finished goods, 5% manufacturing, 12% packaging, 7% logistics, 9% overheads and others.
Donut chart titled "Renewable electricity at our manufacturing sites at year end (%), with a target of 100% by 2025" showing 91.9% in 2023, with comparison values 78.4% in 2022 and 63.7% in 2021.

A photograph of a man outdoors in a forest or plantation setting, wearing a cap and dark polo shirt with yellow trim, measuring the circumference or diameter of a tree trunk with a tape measure. He is leaning in toward the tree and looking intently at the measurement. The background shows many tall trees and green ground cover, suggesting a wooded or agricultural environment. Lighting appears natural daylight. The image is cropped tightly around the man and tree, with no visible text overlays. The photo likely illustrates forestry, sourcing, or environmental assessment context.

Transition risk modeling table with a 10-year horizon and scenarios High, Intermediate, and Low; includes rows for temperature increase by 2100, global action against climate change, business scope, modeling simulations, and modeling metric.

title: Transition risk impacts, mitigations and future opportunities

description: Photograph of a man examining green plants in an indoor agricultural setting, likely a greenhouse or research facility.

World map visualization titled 'Impact of climate and physical risks on Nestlé’s key ingredient yields by 2040'. It shows modeled yield changes by 2040 vs. 2022 for key ingredients across sourcing countries, with a legend for green (+2% increase or more), white (-2% decrease to +2% increase), and red (-2% decrease or more). The map covers North and South America, Europe, Africa, Asia, and Australia, with labeled countries including US, Mexico, Colombia, Ecuador, Chile, Argentina, Brazil, Honduras, Ghana, Côte d’Ivoire, Pakistan, South Africa, Spain, UK, France, Germany, Italy, India, Thailand, Malaysia, Vietnam, Indonesia, China, and Australia. A left-side ingredient legend lists arabica, robusta, palm oil, cocoa, dairy, barley, maize, rice, soy, wheat, sugarcane, and sugarbeet. Callout lines connect country labels to small stacked ingredient markers and colored dots indicating yield-change categories.

Small illustrative visual on the right page associated with the article 'Researching resilient coffee plants'; the crop image is provided only as a placeholder/thumbnail and its contents are not legible in the page image.
Photograph of young green seedlings growing in dark soil, likely illustrating agriculture or climate/renewables context.

Energy consumption in our factories table with columns 2021, 2022, 2023 and rows for energy consumption (gigajoules), energy consumed that is renewable energy (%), and energy consumed that was supplied from grid electricity (%).
